TEXT.source: NTU Corpus stories for Kavalan, story: KavNr-sea_buya
TEXT.citation: Sung, L. M., Lily, I., Hsieh, F., & Lin, Z. (2008). Developing an online corpus of Formosan languages. Taiwan Journal of Linguistics, 6(2.).
TEXT.BibTeX_citation: @article{sung2008developing,title={Developing an online corpus of Formosan languages.},author={Sung, Li-May and Lily, I and Hsieh, Fuhui and Lin, Zhemin and others},journal={Taiwan Journal of Linguistics},volume={6},number={2},year={2008}}

S.FORM: aimi kebalan azu ngid kataz qatiw sa lazing nani.
S.PHON: aimi kəβaran az~ru ŋiɮ kataz~r qatiw sa raz~riŋ nani.
S.FORM: aimi kebalan azu ngid kataz qatiw sa lazing nani.
S.PHON: aimi kəβaran az~ru ŋiɮ kataz~r qatiw sa raz~riŋ nani.
S.TRANSL: 如果我們噶瑪蘭人想去海邊,
If we Kavalan people want to go to the sea,
絕對會找伴 .

TRANSL.notes: (we) will definitely ask friends (to come with us).

S.TRANSL: will definitely ask friends .
一起去海邊比較好
It's better if we go to the sea together.
更能高高興興在那裡.
Being much happier there.
如果到了海邊,
If arrive at the sea,
男人下水抓魚和射魚.
Men dive into the waters, catching and spearing fish.
女人撿笠螺和螺類.
Women pick up limpets and shells.

S.FORM: u yaupama nayi qiqakutung u iza ya.
S.PHON: u jaupama naji qiqakutuŋ u iz~ra ja.
S.FORM: u yaupama nayi qiqakutung u iza ya.
S.PHON: u jaupama naji qiqakutuŋ u iz~ra ja.
S.TRANSL: 或撿海菜.
or pick up seaweed.

S.FORM: nisianguzusanna ngid qan satutu nizanta pazukat.
S.PHON: nisiaŋuz~rusanna ŋiɮ qan satutu niz~ranta paz~rukat.
S.FORM: nisianguzusanna ngid qan satutu nizanta pazukat.
S.PHON: nisiaŋuz~rusanna ŋiɮ qan satutu niz~ranta paz~rukat.
S.TRANSL: 中午要吃飯的時候,在海邊所採的東西全部都拿出來.
We take out all that we have picked up at sea when it is time for lunch.

W.TRANSL: 完成-在海邊撿食物-名物化-3PL.屬格

S.FORM: sa satutuan yau metiay yau maninay.
S.PHON: sa satutuan jau mətiaj jau maninaj.
S.FORM: sa satutuan yau metiay yau maninay.
S.PHON: sa satutuan jau mətiaj jau maninaj.
S.TRANSL: 中午吃的有生的有熟的.
There are raw and cooked food for lunch.

S.FORM: satutuan maiita mazas tu ninuay nitalepawan matiw sa lazing.
S.PHON: satutuan maiita maz~ras tu ninuaj nitarəpawan matiw sa raz~riŋ.
S.FORM: satutuan maiita mazas tu ninuay nitalepawan matiw sa lazing.
S.PHON: satutuan maiita maz~ras tu ninuaj nitarəpawan matiw sa raz~riŋ.
S.TRANSL: 我們不帶家裡的東西去海邊.
We don't take food from home to the sea.

S.FORM: qawman wama zana lazingay tamunniq tayan.
S.PHON: qawman wama z~rana raz~riŋaj tamunniq tajan.
S.FORM: qawman wama zana lazingay tamunniq tayan.
S.PHON: qawman wama z~rana raz~riŋaj tamunniq tajan.
S.TRANSL: 我們的菜絕對只有海邊的菜.
Our foods are definitely only those picked up at the sea.
帶的東西只有飯和鹽.

TRANSL.notes: What are brought (from home) are only rice and salt.

S.TRANSL: What are brought are only rice and salt.

S.FORM: nayau zana kebalan matiw sa lazing.
S.PHON: najau z~rana kəβaran matiw sa raz~riŋ.
S.FORM: nayau zana kebalan matiw sa lazing.
S.PHON: najau z~rana kəβaran matiw sa raz~riŋ.
S.TRANSL: 噶瑪蘭人是那樣子去海邊的.
That is how Kavalan people go to the sea.
使我們高興及好的生活方式.
the way of life which makes us happy and good
我們是這樣想的.
That's how we think.
